title: Level 290
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 290: '''Surreal Relaxation''' }} {{Image Banner|caption=The odd but magnificent ocean view instantly soothes your body...|image=roadsislandf.jpg}} {{Blurbackground|file=unsplshthseb3aches.jpg}} {{Class 1|hexagon=0|color-h=190|class=0|entity=Devoid of Entities|security=Somewhat Stable|safety=Safe}} {{Audio|file=genuinelyagoodsong.ogg|volume=0.6769|secrettext=avian give 10/10 pls but you might not give bc i stole your css HAHAHAAh}} = Overview = ---- {{Blurimage|file=unsplshthseb3aches.jpg|caption=A photo showing the shores of '''Level 290'''.}} '''Level 290''', titled and commonly known as "Surreal Relaxation", is composed of several small islands, being surrounded by a vast lukewarm ocean. The depths of the ocean reaches up to 30 meters (98.4 feet) long, with its average depth amounting to 12.7 meters (41.7 feet). The level is most known for its attractive ocean views, unnaturally clear water, and its beaches. Due to this, Level 290 is a common destination among wanderers in the Backrooms. All of the islands are generally characterized by tall palm trees standing on top of dirt-layered rocks. The palm trees either crowd to the point of a forest appearance, or sparsely placed. Its shores are always made up of smooth, frictionless sand, to the point where tripping or quicksand formation can seldomly happen. Small unexpected bumps, rocks or tree branches within pathways can cause even more accidents to occur, so caution should be exercised during exploration. The magnificent, clear ocean is the most famous part of '''Level 290'''. The water is impossibly clear; its quality comparable to glass. The temperature inside the ocean is roughly 35°C, although it could fluctuate. Travelers can get healed by soaking themselves in the ocean, as it has anomalous healing properties. It is unknown how the water remains completely transparent after being exposed to blood, but it also may contain self-cleaning properties. Another aspect of '''Level 290''' that made the level famous is that it keeps cortisol levels extremely low, to the point that it may be considered entirely absent. Epinephrine and other hormones associated or related to it is also kept in low levels, although not as low as the cortisol levels. Air quality inside the level is inexplicably higher than most levels in the Backrooms; breathing in this air even temporarily heals lung dieases such as cancer or pnuemonia. The extreme air purity also somehow affects the temperature of this level, as the temperature maintains a temperature of 22°C, opposed to the fact that usual air found in [[The Frontrooms]] would normally be 35°C. It is suspected that the temperature is managed by an anomaly instead, but it is yet to be confirmed. Wooden lounge chairs are frequently and consistently seen throughout all islands; their origin are indeterminate. Piles of furniture is also often found inside '''Level 290'''; it is not recommended to keep furniture from these heaps as they can disappear without a trace. However, items that remain on the ocean like jetskis or boats peculiarly do not demanifest; it is unknown why the level does this. Moreover, this anomaly makes it highly suspectible to floating trash in the sea,The self-cleaning property appears to only affect particulates, foreign gas and liquid, with normal-sized items like water bottles unable to be eliminated. hence volunteering to clean is appreciated and encouraged. Wooden huts are commonly found during exploration and they can be made into makeshift homes. The huts are usually small in size, as they are only able to accommodate one to two wanderers. Doors are completely absent within the shacks, making privacy harder to manage. Hidden splinters inside huts are also becoming increasingly frequent within the past few years. == Islands == There are a total of four islands in this level, all of which are deemed safe to inhabit, travel, and explore. They vary in size and shape, but one notable characteristic from all of them is that they have unique features, with the exception of Selrufo Island. The islands are placed in a line, and the distance between them is quite vast. {{Blurimage|file=roadsislandf.jpg|caption=A picture of Roads Island.}} '''Roads Island''', not to be confused with Rhodes Island from [[The Frontrooms]], is the biggest island present in '''Level 290'''; it is also well-known for having natural asphalt roads inside its area. Moreover, it houses the most population out of all islands, with permanent residences and buildings constructed on the island. The asphalt roads appears to be crusty and dry, but research suggest an anomaly present inside the asphalt material; that is absorbing water unnaturally quick compared to standard materials. Further studies show that the material making up the roads is not asphalt—the unknown material works and appears nearly identical to normal asphalt. Footwear is also recommend when traversing on these pathways, as it has tiny spikes that cannot be seen to the naked eye that can cause mild injuries. '''Emedaché Island''' is known to house a plethora of diverse environments. Notably, multiple climates co-exist, as some areas' climates can abruptly lead to the other climates. Flora thrives in this island due to the diverse ecosystems. The flora are not considered entities as they pose no harm and do not have intelligence. '''Tweurck Island''' is not extensively explored compared to other islets due to unforeseen circumstances, thus leaving it barely visited. However, one noteworthy feature of this islet is that it is completely devoid of any form of trees. Multiple patches of grass are abnormally different from others in terms of temperature and color. It is unknown why the grass functions as such. '''Selrufo Island''' is recognized as the smallest island present in the level. Despite being visited frequently, permanent residents remains few to none, mostly due to the island's miniscule size. As of present, it does not have a unique or deriative feature unlike other islets. = Entities = ---- '''Level 290''' is devoid of any entities. Although there are reports of tiny fish swimming around Tweurck Island, this is unconfirmed. = Addendum = ----
Recovered Note
During my time in exploration, I find myself in constant peace. Relaxing waves wash over each other, the level mimicking seagull sounds. I have never experienced such tranquility in a level before. A few months pass, and '''Level 290''' has became my new home. I figure I might as well join a community, no? And so, I decided to help out M.E.G., specifically, Base Reform. I was assigned to aid in rehabilitating Tweurck Island, with a few other nice people. As soon as I stepped on the island, I felt a sense of strangeness coming off the islet, though small. I thought I was being dramatic, so I helped the team with moving items off the boat. We started to explore the unexplored, clearing off the small branches to the side. But where was the branches coming from? Didn't the database say there were no trees at all? This made it feel all the more eerie. Anyhow, my teammates told me to ignore it, so I did. Moving deeper into the island, Our team was immediately attacked by a foul smell. We cautiously and slowly moved towards where the scent came from, and saw massive pulps of blood, flesh, and wooden parts and that was... I don't know, like, amalgamated into each other, I guess? We absolutely horrified by the scene, called backup, had to go back to our boat and mark it 'explored'. We had no idea what was going on at that island, but it was pretty fuckin' scary. Right before I got on the boat, I saw some fish in the ocean. That's a first, there weren't any entities, as they said. I pictured it and admired them, I'll send them to the higher-ups if I remember; just wishing that I do.
- note from a [[Phenomenon 25|Hangman's Noose]] victim

= Communities = ---- '''M.E.G. Base Reform''' * 210+ members * Open to trade * Main rehabilitator on all islands '''Perasor''' * 28 members * Closed to trade, free to join * Tourist agency '''DF Tours''' * 23 members * Open to trade * Tourist agency '''SSR (Settlers of Surreal Relaxation)''' * ~50 members * Open to trade, free to join * Regular outpost = Entrances and Exits = ---- === Entrances === Seeing multiple islands occasionally in [[Level 100]] and swimming there will lead here;
Finding sand in [[Level 399]]'s Neon Natatorium and [[Level 119]] will instantly transport one here;
In [[Level 10]], wandering for an extended period of time will seamlessly lead here.
=== Exits === Swimming deep into the ocean until you cannot see the islands anymore will slowly fade into [[Level 7]];
Randomly no-clipping can send one to a [[Level List|random level]];
Entering an unusually bright yellow restaurant can transport to [[Level 167]].
No-clipping into any asphalt road on '''Roads Island''' will teleport one to [[Level 303]].
